description_long: This object is a circular, multi-layered structure composed of interlocking, wedge-shaped stone blocks arranged in three stacked tiers. The blocks are uniformly shaped and tightly fitted, creating a visually striking, geometric pattern with alternating seams. The structure is hollow in the center, forming a low, open enclosure reminiscent of a well or planter. The stone blocks have a smooth, matte finish with subtle wood-like grain patterns and a light brown coloration. One block is shown separated from the main structure, highlighting its individual form and modularity. The overall construction suggests durability and a design intended to resist shifting or collapse.
description: A circular, stacked arrangement of interlocking, wedge-shaped stone blocks forming a low, open enclosure with a smooth, light brown finish.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the structure from a slightly elevated angle, revealing the open center and the staggered, interlocking pattern of the blocks, with one block separated to the right.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a direct front view, emphasizing the symmetry of the stacked blocks and the open entryway formed by a single block at the base.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the structure from the opposite side of view 0, again showing the open center and the separated block now on the left.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a rear view, highlighting the continuous, uninterrupted circular arrangement of the blocks with no visible entry gap.
attribute_location_context: urban park landscaping or decorative public plaza
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or discoloration.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: interlocked with neighboring blocks to form a continuous circular enclosure
